title: Intense Herbal Kenyan Chicken Tagine
description: A high-protein Kenyan dish featuring herbes-de-provence, perfect for a post-workout meal, cooked in a traditional tajine clay vessel to reduce and intensify the flavors. This recipe combines the warmth of African spices with the richness of herbs to create a truly unique culinary experience.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in the tajine clay vessel over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is translucent.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger and cook for 1 minute.
Add the chicken pieces and cook until they are browned on all sides.
Add the herbes-de-provence, chicken broth, and dried apricots to the vessel.
Cover the vessel with a lid and bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer for 30 minutes.
Season the tagine with salt and pepper to taste.
Serve the tagine hot, garnished with fresh herbs if desired.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
